title: Scotland Fans Drank Boston Out of Beer
script: Boston out of beer? The Tartan Army rolled into town for the 2026 World Cup — kilts on, pints in hand — and within days, Boston bars ran dry. The Samuel Adams taproom alone sold 4,000 pints of Boston Lager in four days. That's roughly 90 empty kegs. The White Bull Tavern? No beer. The Phoenix Landing ran through 20 of its 24 draft lines and started receiving emergency beer deliveries while returning 50 empty kegs to distributors. Tennent's Lager — basically Scotland's national drink — suddenly appeared at 76 Boston locations. Bar workers were restocking around the clock. A city that survived the American Revolution, two Red Sox collapses, and three Patriots dynasties finally met its match: Scottish soccer fans. So: will Boston stock Tennent's from now on?
